attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal or vote submission data

security_gap.gap_description: User identity verification is critical for authorized governance participation.

actor_risk.description: User needs to prove ownership/control of BGT to participate in governance.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed identity verification could allow unauthorized governance actions.

assumptions: The mechanism for verifying a user's identity or BGT ownership for governance actions is insufficient.
An attacker can create messages appearing to originate from a legitimate user.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and signature verification for all governance transactions.

description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to submit fraudulent governance proposals or votes.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal or vote submission data

security_gap.gap_description: Integrity of governance data in transit or before on-chain finality.

actor_risk.description: User's communication or input channel could be compromised.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data integrity checks are necessary to prevent manipulation of governance inputs.

assumptions: The channel used to submit governance actions is vulnerable to modification.
Transaction signatures or data integrity checks are missing or insufficient.

mitigation.strategy: Use secure communication channels (HTTPS) and require transaction signatures covering all critical data.

description: An attacker intercepts and modifies a user's governance proposal or vote before it is processed by the contract.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal or vote submission data

security_gap.gap_description: The governance system needs protection against resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user can send large volumes of requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming the governance contract can disrupt the process.

assumptions: The cost of submitting governance transactions (gas) is low relative to the attack cost.
The governance contract lacks effective rate limiting or spam prevention logic.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure appropriate transaction fees and implement contract-level checks if necessary.

description: An attacker floods the governance contract with excessive proposals or votes to disrupt the process.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal or vote submission data

security_gap.gap_description: Privileges must be correctly enforced based on BGT holdings and rules.

actor_risk.description: User could gain unauthorized influence if privilege checks fail.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting privilege checks could manipulate governance outcomes.

assumptions: The governance contract incorrectly calculates or verifies a user's voting power.
An attacker can bypass the required BGT threshold for proposal submission.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously audit contract logic for BGT balance, delegation, and privilege checks.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the governance contract to gain unauthorized voting power or propose without meeting requirements.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation actions require verification of user identity and BGT ownership.

actor_risk.description: User needs to prove ownership/control of BGT to delegate.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed identity verification could allow unauthorized BGT delegation.

assumptions: The mechanism for verifying a user's identity or BGT ownership for delegation is insufficient.
An attacker can create messages appearing to originate from a legitimate user.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and signature verification for all delegation transactions.

description: An attacker impersonates a user to delegate their $BGT to a validator chosen by the attacker.

security_gap.gap_description: Integrity of delegation data in transit.

actor_risk.description: User's communication or input channel could be compromised.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data integrity checks are necessary to prevent manipulation of delegation inputs.

assumptions: The channel used to submit delegation actions is vulnerable to modification.
Transaction signatures or data integrity checks are missing or insufficient for delegation parameters.

mitigation.strategy: Use secure communication channels (HTTPS) and require transaction signatures covering all critical data.

description: An attacker intercepts and modifies a user's $BGT delegation request before it is processed by the contract, changing the target validator or amount.

security_gap.gap_description: The delegation system needs protection against resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user can send large volumes of requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming delegation contract can disrupt boosting for others.

assumptions: The cost of submitting delegation transactions (gas) is low relative to the attack cost.
The delegation contract lacks effective rate limiting or spam prevention logic.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure appropriate transaction fees and implement contract-level checks if necessary.

description: An attacker floods the validator delegation contract with excessive requests to prevent legitimate boosting.

security_gap.gap_description: Delegation contract must correctly verify BGT balance and allowances.

actor_risk.description: User could delegate BGT they don't own if checks fail.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed balance/allowance checks could allow theft or unauthorized delegation.

assumptions: The delegation contract does not correctly verify the user's BGT balance.
The delegation contract does not correctly verify required token allowances (e.g., via transferFrom).

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously audit contract logic for BGT balance and allowance checks during delegation.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability to delegate more $BGT than they own or are authorized to spend.

security_gap.gap_description: Claiming rewards requires proving entitlement linked to user identity.

actor_risk.description: User needs to prove entitlement to claim rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed proof verification could allow claiming rewards belonging to others.

assumptions: The mechanism for generating or verifying claim proofs is flawed.
An attacker can obtain or forge a valid proof for another user's rewards.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously audit the proof generation and on-chain verification logic. Ensure proof uniqueness.

description: An attacker obtains or forges a valid claim proof and impersonates a user to steal their earned rewards.

security_gap.gap_description: Integrity of claim request and proof data in transit.

actor_risk.description: User's communication channel or claim data could be compromised.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data integrity checks are necessary to prevent manipulation of claim requests.

assumptions: The channel used to submit claim requests is vulnerable to modification.
Transaction signatures or data integrity checks are missing or insufficient for claim data.

mitigation.strategy: Use secure communication channels (HTTPS) and ensure on-chain contract fully validates claim data and proof.

description: An attacker intercepts and modifies a user's reward claim request or proof before it is processed by the contract.

security_gap.gap_description: The reward claim system needs protection against resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user can send large volumes of requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming the claim contract can prevent legitimate users from claiming.

assumptions: The cost of submitting claim transactions (gas) is low relative to the attack cost.
The claim contract lacks effective rate limiting or spam prevention logic.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure appropriate transaction fees and implement contract-level checks if necessary.

description: An attacker floods the reward claim contract with excessive requests, preventing legitimate users from claiming their rewards.

security_gap.gap_description: Claim contract must accurately verify proof against entitlement and prevent double-claiming.

actor_risk.description: User could claim more rewards than entitled if proof logic is flawed.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed claim logic could allow stealing or over-claiming rewards.

assumptions: The claim contract does not correctly verify the amount entitled by the proof.
The claim contract does not prevent a user from claiming the same rewards multiple times.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously audit claim contract logic for proof verification and tracking of claimed amounts.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the reward claim contract to claim more rewards than they are entitled to or claim the same rewards multiple times.

security_gap.gap_description: Redeeming BGT requires proving ownership and control of the tokens.

actor_risk.description: User needs to prove ownership/control of BGT to redeem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed identity verification could allow unauthorized BGT redemption and BERA theft.

assumptions: The mechanism for verifying a user's identity or BGT ownership for redemption is insufficient.
An attacker can create messages appearing to originate from a legitimate user.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and signature verification for all redemption transactions.

description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to burn their $BGT and steal the resulting $BERA tokens.

security_gap.gap_description: Integrity of redemption data in transit.

actor_risk.description: User's communication or input channel could be compromised.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Data integrity checks are necessary to prevent manipulation of redemption inputs.

assumptions: The channel used to submit redemption requests is vulnerable to modification.
Transaction signatures or data integrity checks are missing or insufficient for redemption parameters.

mitigation.strategy: Use secure communication channels (HTTPS) and require transaction signatures covering all critical data.

description: An attacker intercepts and modifies a user's $BGT redemption request before it is processed by the contract, changing the amount.

security_gap.gap_description: The redemption system needs protection against resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: Malicious user can send large volumes of requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming the redemption contract can prevent legitimate users from redeeming.

assumptions: The cost of submitting redemption transactions (gas) is low relative to the attack cost.
The redemption contract lacks effective rate limiting or spam prevention logic.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure appropriate transaction fees and implement contract-level checks if necessary.

description: An attacker floods the BGT redemption contract with excessive requests, preventing legitimate users from converting their $BGT to $BERA.

security_gap.gap_description: Redemption contract must correctly verify BGT balance/allowance and calculate BERA amount.

actor_risk.description: User could burn BGT they don't own or get excess BERA if checks fail.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flawed balance/allowance checks or calculation could allow theft or supply inflation.

assumptions: The redemption contract does not correctly verify the user's BGT balance or allowance before burning.
The redemption contract incorrectly calculates the amount of BERA to be transferred.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously audit contract logic for BGT balance/allowance checks and BERA calculation.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the redemption contract to burn $BGT they do not own or receive more $BERA than entitled.
